participants.name: Ted Hurst
participants.notes: Primary prospect at pro day. Chose to stand on his NFL Combine numbers (4.42 forty, 36.5" vertical, 135" broad jump) and only did positional drills. Combine measurements used: 6-3 7/8, 206 lbs, 32 5/8" arms, 9 5/8" hands. Senior Bowl attendee. Transferred from Valdosta State (D-II). All-Sun Belt 1st Team 2025. Did not re-test at pro day.

additional_notes: Georgia State went 1-11 in 2025, severely limiting the number of draft-eligible prospects. Ted Hurst (WR) was the only Georgia State player invited to the 2026 NFL Scouting Combine and was the primary focus of the pro day according to available reporting. Hurst elected to stand on his combine numbers and participated only in positional drills at the pro day. Specific testing results from the pro day itself were not publicly reported for any participant. Multiple NFL teams scouted the event, including the Washington Commanders. It is likely that additional Georgia State seniors participated in measurements and/or drills at the pro day (as is typical even for small programs), but no results for other participants were found in any publicly available source despite exhaustive searching across NFL.com, ESPN, PFF, 247Sports, georgiastatesports.com, NFL Draft Buzz, Pro Football Network, Yahoo Sports, and beat reporter coverage. The NFL.com pro day schedule listed Georgia State's pro day date as March 11, 2026, though Draft Insiders' Digest listed it as March 18 — the March 11 date from NFL.com is used here as the more authoritative source.
